description:
You are a site administrator who wants to audit last month’s user-account activity.  
All raw data has already been collected for you in the file  

    /home/user/logs/user_mgmt.log  

Your goal is to extract only the “ADD” and “DEL” events that happened during **October 2023** (from `2023-10-01 00:00:00` through `2023-10-31 23:59:59`, inclusive).  
Follow these steps and naming conventions exactly; the automated tests will verify every character.

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/output  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Produce an **exact copy** (byte-for-byte) of every qualifying log line, preserving the original order found in *user_mgmt.log*, and save them to  

   /home/user/output/oct2023_user_changes.log  

   • The file must end with a trailing newline.  
   • No extra blank lines or spaces may be introduced.  
   • Only lines whose timestamp is inside October 2023 **and** whose action field is “ADD” or “DEL” must appear.

3. From the file generated in step 2, create a concise CSV summary named  

   /home/user/output/oct2023_user_changes_summary.csv  

   The CSV must have:

   • A single header row exactly:  
     `username,action,date,time`  

   • One subsequent row per line in *oct2023_user_changes.log*.  
   • Columns explained:  
     – username  → value that follows `user=` in the log line  
     – action    → literal “ADD” or “DEL” (uppercase)  
     – date      → `YYYY-MM-DD` part of the timestamp  
     – time      → `HH:MM:SS` part of the timestamp (24-hour clock)  

   • Rows must be **sorted alphabetically by the username column** (case-sensitive A→Z).  
   • Use a comma **without** surrounding spaces as the only delimiter.  
   • The file must end with a trailing newline and contain no blank lines.

4. Leave all other files untouched.

The grader will:

• Confirm that /home/user/output exists.  
• Diff /home/user/output/oct2023_user_changes.log against the expected set of five October lines.  
• Diff /home/user/output/oct2023_user_changes_summary.csv against the exact expected CSV (header plus five rows, sorted as specified).

Complete the task entirely from the command line; do NOT edit files manually with a text editor.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):

1. Directory structure:
   /home/user/
   ├── logs/
   │   └── user_mgmt.log
   └── (no “output” directory yet)

2. File: /home/user/logs/user_mgmt.log  
   Contents (including final newline):

[2023-09-30 23:59:59] INFO system: ADD user=alice id=1001
[2023-10-01 00:00:01] INFO system: ADD user=bob id=1002
[2023-10-05 12:34:56] INFO system: DEL user=charlie id=1003
[2023-10-15 08:00:00] INFO system: ADD user=david id=1004
[2023-10-20 14:15:30] INFO system: DEL user=alice id=1001
[2023-10-31 23:59:59] INFO system: ADD user=erin id=1005
[2023-11-01 00:00:01] INFO system: ADD user=frank id=1006

3. Expected files after successful task completion:

A. /home/user/output/oct2023_user_changes.log  
   Expected content (exact, including trailing newline):

[2023-10-01 00:00:01] INFO system: ADD user=bob id=1002
[2023-10-05 12:34:56] INFO system: DEL user=charlie id=1003
[2023-10-15 08:00:00] INFO system: ADD user=david id=1004
[2023-10-20 14:15:30] INFO system: DEL user=alice id=1001
[2023-10-31 23:59:59] INFO system: ADD user=erin id=1005

B. /home/user/output/oct2023_user_changes_summary.csv  
   Expected content (exact, including trailing newline):

username,action,date,time
alice,DEL,2023-10-20,14:15:30
bob,ADD,2023-10-01,00:00:01
charlie,DEL,2023-10-05,12:34:56
david,ADD,2023-10-15,08:00:00
erin,ADD,2023-10-31,23:59:59

4. Permissions: the agent runs as the owner of /home/user, so creation of /home/user/output and new files within is permitted without elevation.

5. The grading script will:
   • Verify the existence and exact content (using sha256sum) of the two output files.
   • Check that no extraneous files matching oct2023_user_changes* exist outside /home/user/output.
name: task_000000_74b01700
